title: Possibility of Rebuild Screenings Worldwide
wikitext:
[]: Possibility of Rebuild Screenings Worldwide?             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Nov 03, 2012 11:05 pm Do not be alarmed by the title of this thread. It's not actually happening. I thought about putting this in the "Speculation Thread" or the "Puella Magi Madoka Magica" thread, but I decided to make a new one since I didn't want to create tangents in either. (Not that the Speculation Thread isn't full of tangents anyway....) I was reading up on the Puella Magi Madoka Magica movies, specifically the fact that the first two movies were screened (with subs) at five locations in the US, in addition to 13 other locations worldwide. Thankfully I was in Baltimore, MD visiting a college with my parents while the New York City screenings were going on; I would have been severely pissed off if I'd missed out on a chance to see those movies.... Tangent aside, a thought occurred to me: Since NGE has gained a massive following outside of Japan, does it surprise anyone else that they haven't done something similar with the Rebuild movies? It would make my year to be able to see a sub of Rebuild here in the States. Anyone think there's a chance they might do this for 4.0?

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Sun Nov 04, 2012 8:13 am For 1.0 and 2.0 we have had showings here in Canada, (unfortunately dubbed and long after the original release date). It would be great to be able to see them earlier and with the proper subs; and you are right, since rebuild Eva has gained a much greater following outside of Japan (not that is was low to begin with) so I think it's perfectly possible they would do this for 4.0 Still seems slim but I'm hoping.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Nov 04, 2012 9:07 am I saw 2.0 in theaters subbed. I don't know anywhere that played it dubbed, as I was under the impression that Funimation just hadn't finished the English dub yet, but was going to release anyway. I'm kind of a fan of Hayashibara and Yamadera, so any film where I actually get to hear both talents in it is a great thing for me. (They seem to share a lot of shows and movies with each other too. How awesomely coincidental...)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Nov 04, 2012 9:12 am Closest we'd get is various world festival releases (during? and after the time of 3.0 in Japanese theaters) and Funimation's theatrical releases before the DVD/BD release (ala 1.0 and 2.0). I'd love a simultaneous/worldwide film release, but I don't think it'd happen. Though it'd be nice to be pleasantly surprised for either Q or Final.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sun Nov 04, 2012 9:26 am I think Funi was screening 2.0 in Los Angeles unfortunately I missed the opportunity,other than that I've only heard of it premiering internationally in a film festival in Canada. Like everyone else I think a simultaneous international release would be cool but it's very unlikely to ever happen.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Nov 04, 2012 9:31 am 2.0 did screen subbed in Canada at THIS FESTIVAL back in November 2009 but no such luck this time it seems. I wish I had a ticket like this again but adjusted for Q http://i.imgur.com/gluKe.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Chiral_Spiral » Sun Nov 04, 2012 10:51 am I saw 2.0 at an anime festival in Dublin in 2009, quite soon after the Japanese release if I recall and way before it came out on DVD or Blu Ray in English speaking regions. There's no evidence that they'll do another one any time soon but I'm hoping they might, since there have been quite a number of high profile anime movies released in the interim (Letters to Momo, new Makoto Shinkai movie, Wolf Children, maybe even the Madoka movies) so there's be no shortage of material. Plus the cinema that put it on seems to have at least a few anime fans on staff, as they show anime there quite regularly. My advice to everyone would be to keep an eye on your local arthouse or specialty cinema, particularly if they've shown movies like this before. The energy in the room for 2.0 really added to the experience, with everyone cheering and laughing at the in-jokes and gasping at all the new stuff. Definitely the ideal way to see a movie like this, IMO. Edit: Speak of the devil, I typed "Evangelion 3.0 Ireland" into google and apparently the people who provided the movies for the festival I just mentioned are exploring the possibility of a screening here in Spring.

[]:             Quote Post by sweet-d » Sun Nov 04, 2012 11:43 pm basically if you get lucky you might see it in at a film festival or con sometime early next year. but I would think it would be shown at least some at a few places soon after release in Japan. I've seen anime movies shown at art museums shortly after being released in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by DemonSpade » Mon Nov 05, 2012 9:01 am Sooooo.... I think 1.0 and 2.0 were screened at festivals here in Germany. Which sites should I check to find out if this will be the case with 3.0 as well? I'd die to see this on the big screen

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Mon Nov 05, 2012 9:13 am DemonSpade wrote: Sooooo.... I think 1.0 and 2.0 were screened at festivals here in Germany. Which sites should I check to find out if this will be the case with 3.0 as well? I'd die to see this on the big screen

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Nov 05, 2012 11:18 am I wasn't able to make it to 2.0 theatrical release. But I have plans on seeing 3.0 when it hits theaters here, and nothing in the universe will impede my progress.

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Mon Nov 05, 2012 3:27 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: I wasn't able to make it to 2.0 theatrical release. But I have plans on seeing 3.0 when it hits theaters here, and nothing in the universe will impede my progress.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Mon Nov 05, 2012 3:48 pm I saw 1.0 at a small film festival thingy in the city where they also showed other films like Godzilla and some other Tokusatsu/Kaiju film I don't remember
